videos.title: Angle Basics | What Are Angles? | Math Antics
videos.description: Learn the basics of angles in geometry! Understand what an angle is, how two lines meet to form an angle, and discover the different types of angles: right angles, acute angles, obtuse angles, and straight angles.
learningObjectives: Learn 4 types of angles: right, acute, obtuse, straight
keyConceptsCovered: Angle = space between two lines that meet at a point

videos.title: Types of Angles Song | Acute, Obtuse & Right Angles | NUMBEROCK
videos.description: Join Earl the pizza delivery man on his route and discover different types of angles! Learn to spot acute angles (like the top of a pizza slice), right angles (like the corner of a square), and obtuse angles (like clock hands at quarter to two).
learningObjectives: Reinforce angle types through a fun song
keyConceptsCovered: Acute = sharp like pizza slice tip
Right = square corner like a book

videos.title: Right Angles Around Us | Finding 90 Degree Angles
videos.description: Look for right angles everywhere! The corner of a book, the corner of a door, the corner of a window - right angles are all around us. Learn to use the corner of a piece of paper to check for right angles.
learningObjectives: Identify right angles in everyday objects
Use a paper corner as a right-angle checker
keyConceptsCovered: Right angles in book corners, doors, windows
Using paper corner to test for right angles
Most man-made objects have right angles
followUpTopics: Identifying acute and obtuse in real life

videos.title: Angle Types Practice | Identify Acute, Right, and Obtuse
videos.description: Practice identifying different angle types! Look at various angles and decide if each one is acute (sharp), right (square corner), or obtuse (wide). A fun interactive quiz to test your understanding.
keyConceptsCovered: Classifying angles as acute, right, or obtuse

videos.title: Angles in Shapes | Finding Angles in Triangles and Rectangles
videos.description: Discover angles inside shapes! Find out how many right angles a rectangle has, what kinds of angles a triangle can have, and how to spot angles in everyday objects like clocks and scissors.
learningObjectives: Count right angles in rectangles and squares
keyConceptsCovered: Triangles can have acute, right, or obtuse angles

excellent_performance.description: For students who scored >= 90% on readiness
excellent_performance.rationale: Fast-track through angles with real-world application and angles in shapes

ready_performance.description: For students who scored 80-89% on readiness
ready_performance.rationale: Complete coverage of angles with practice and application

partial_readiness.description: For students who scored 50-79% on readiness
partial_readiness.rationale: Core angle concepts after prerequisite review

after_prerequisite_review.description: For students who scored < 50% but completed prerequisites

questions.question: The corner of a book makes what type of angle?
questions.explanation: The corner of a book is a right angle (90 degrees). It forms a perfect square corner. You can check by placing the corner of a piece of paper against it - they match!

usageNotes.teacherGuidance: Use the corner of a piece of paper as a right-angle checker. Have students walk around the classroom and classify angles they find as acute, right, or obtuse.
usageNotes.parentGuidance: Open and close a pair of scissors slowly to show how angles change from acute to right to obtuse. Look at clock hands at different times to find different angle types.
usageNotes.studentGuidance: Use the corner of your notebook as a tool! Hold it against any corner you see. If it matches perfectly, it is a right angle. If it is smaller, it is acute. If it is bigger, it is obtuse.
